title: Untrusted Assets, Save Parsing, Paths, Privacy, and Local Security · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers untrusted assets, save parsing, paths, privacy, and local security into a changed bounded local-data and privacy policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded local-data and privacy policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences.
Constrain asset, settings, replay, and score paths to approved roots and reject traversal, symlink, type, and size violations.
Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Threat Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 3462 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Threat Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for identify protected player data and availability, untrusted files and paths, package supply chain, local attackers, and realistic consequences. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Threat Model, keep Threat Model connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Privacy Telemetry
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-privacy-telemetry-2zop1e" that uses case 4820 to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Privacy Telemetry.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Privacy Telemetry into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Privacy Telemetry, keep Privacy Telemetry connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-privacy-telemetry-2zop1e
# Competency: asteroids-privacy-telemetry.
# Case 4820: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_privacy_telemetry_2zop1e(untrusted_paths: int = 6, rejected_paths: int = 6, save_size: int = 4096, save_limit: int = 65536, remote_content: bool = False, telemetry_opt_in: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_2zop1e = "2zop1e-e1poz2-4820"
    assert evidence_variant_2zop1e
    evidence_axes_2zop1e = (
        "player2zop1e",
        "seed4820",
        "inpute1poz2",
        "frame2zop1e4820",
        "state48202zop1e",
        "playteste1poz24820",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_2zop1e)) == 6
    if untrusted_paths < 0 or rejected_paths < 0:
        return False, rejected_paths, "security-domain"
    if rejected_paths != untrusted_paths:
        return False, rejected_paths, "path-admission"
    if save_size < 0 or save_size > save_limit:
        return False, save_size, "save-budget"
    if remote_content or not telemetry_opt_in:
        return False, save_size, "external-data"
    assert save_size <= save_limit
    return True, rejected_paths, "bounded-local-data"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Privacy Telemetry
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 7414 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Minimize diagnostics, redact paths and identifiers, require informed opt-in for telemetry, and document retention and deletion.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
